  1. de.fhdwhannover.integrea.ga.core

    1. evaluate(Individual)

Schnittstelle FitnessFunction<T>

Typparameter:
T - The gene type used in the individual's chromosomes (e.g., Integer, Character).
Alle bekannten Implementierungsklassen:
BinaryFitnessFunction, CryptanalysisFitnessFunction, KnapsackFitnessFunction, SudokuFitnessFunction, SymRegFitnessFunction, TSPFitnessFunction
public interface FitnessFunction<T>
Contract for evaluating the mathematical quality of a given candidate solution.

Implementations of this interface translate the raw genetic material of an Individual into an objective performance score, which the Genetic Algorithm uses to dictate survival and selection.

  • Methodenübersicht

    Modifikator und Typ
    Methode
    Beschreibung
    int
    evaluate(Individual<T> individual)
    Evaluates a candidate individual against problem-specific domain constraints.

  • Methodendetails

    • evaluate

      int evaluate(Individual<T> individual)
      Evaluates a candidate individual against problem-specific domain constraints.

      Constraint: The returned integer must represent a maximization goal (where higher is better) and must be floored at 0 to prevent selection algorithms (like Roulette) from crashing on negative boundaries. If a problem natively minimizes (like measuring error), the result must be inverted and scaled up by this method before returning.

      Parameter:
      individual - The specific candidate solution containing the generated chromosomes.
      Gibt zurück:
      A non-negative integer representing the individual's fitness score.
